text: What absolute per-run timeout should gate the native concurrent corpus?
why_it_matters: The Charter requires a domain timeout but supplies no number; it controls hang detection, CI duration, and teardown escalation.
unlocks: CORP-FR-004, CORP-FR-009, CORP-NFR-001, CORP-NFR-005, and the 100-run reliability command.

options.label: 10 seconds
options.pros: Generous for shared CI while still bounding hangs.
options.cons: A pathological 100-run failure can still consume substantial time.

default: Use a 10-second absolute deadline from parent launch through cleanup, with graceful teardown using at most 2 seconds of the remaining deadline before forced kill.

answer.text: Option 1: use a 10-second absolute deadline from parent launch through cleanup, with graceful teardown consuming at most 2 seconds of the remaining deadline before forced kill.
